zh.lifeSummary: 當Sidney James Edwin Sargent於1408年12月12日出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾庫廷哈爾時，他的父親Edwin Sargent III26歲，母親Frances Morgan27歲。他於1432年與Eliza Braybrook在英國諾薩普頓史爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1509年5月14日在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾庫廷哈爾享年100歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sidney James Edwin Sargent于1408年12月12日出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾庫廷哈爾时，他的父亲Edwin Sargent III26岁，母亲Frances Morgan27岁。他于1432年与Eliza Braybrook在英国諾薩普頓史爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1509年5月14日在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾庫廷哈爾享年100岁。

nameMeanings.name: Sargent
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status or occupational name from Middle English sergeaunt ‘servant’ (Old French sergent, serjant). The surname probably originated for the most part in this sense, but the word also developed a number of more specialized meanings in the medieval period, including ‘tenant by military service below the rank of knight’. It was also used for a variety of administrative and legal offices, which may have contributed to the development of the surname. The sense ‘non-commissioned officer’ did not arise until the 16th century. Compare Sergent .

History: William Sargent (1624–1717) came to Gloucester, MA, from Devon, England before 1678. Many of his descendants distinguished themselves in the civil and military affairs of the colonies and some in literary or artistic paths, notably the portrait painter John Singer Sargent (1856–1925).

variants.name: Sergent
meanings: French: status or occupational name from Old French sergent, serjant ‘servant’ (see Sargent ).
Americanized form of Jewish (from Poland) Sierżant: artificial name meaning ‘sergeant’ (senior noncommissioned officer in the Polish infantry).

variants.name: Surgent
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Anatole, Emile.
Slovak: probably a nickname derived from Latin surgens, the present participle of surgo ‘to rise, to get up, to arise’.
French: probably a derivative of Surge, an occupational name from Old French surge ‘surgeon’.

nameMeanings.name: Sidney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which is usually said to be a Norman baronial name from Saint-Denis in France. However, at least in the case of the family of the poet and soldier Sir Philip Sidney ( 1554–86 ), it appears to have a more humble origin, being derived from lands in Surrey named as the ‘wide meadow’ (Old English sīdan ‘wide’ (dative case) + ēg ‘island in a river, riverside meadow’). Evidence of use as a given name dates back to the 18th century, and the popularity of the boy's name increased considerably in the 19th century, probably due in part to Sydney Carton , hero of Dickens's novel A Tale of Two Cities ( 1859 ). As a girl's name it arose in part as a contracted form of Sidony ; coincidentally it represents an altered form of Sindy . In recent years, the girls' name, usually written Sydney , has become popular throughout the English-speaking world, especially in North America.

variants.name: Sydney
meanings: Variant spelling of Sidney . (It was a medieval practice to write y for i, for greater clarity since i was easily confused with other letters.) Although traditionally a boy's name, it suddenly came into fashion as a girl's name at the beginning of the 21st century and, in this form, is now used more frequently for girls than boys in England and North America.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

nameMeanings.name: Edwin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + wine ‘friend’. It was borne by a 7th-century king of Northumbria, who was converted to Christianity by St Paulinus and was killed in battle against pagan forces, a combination of circumstances which led to his being venerated as a martyr. The name occurs occasionally from the mid 16th century, but in modern use it is largely the result of a 19th-century revival.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sidney James Edwin Sargent was born on 12 December 1408, in Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, England, his father, Edwin Sargent III, was 26 and his mother, Frances Morgan, was 27. He married Eliza Braybrook in 1432, in Northamptonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died on 14 May 1509, in Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 100.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sidney James Edwin Sargent est né le 12 décembre 1408, à Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Edwin Sargent III, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Frances Morgan, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Eliza Braybrook en 1432, à Northamptonshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé le 14 mai 1509, à Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 100 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sidney James Edwin Sargent am 12. Dezember 1408 in Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Edwin Sargent III 26 und seine Mutter Frances Morgan 27. Er hat 1432 in Northamptonshire, England Eliza Braybrook geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb am 14. Mai 1509 im Alter von 100 Jahren in Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sidney James Edwin Sargent è nato il il 12 dicembre 1408 nel Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, England, il suo padre, Edwin Sargent III, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Frances Morgan, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Eliza Braybrook nel 1432, nel Northamptonshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì il 14 maggio 1509, nel Courteenhall, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 100 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sidney James Edwin Sargent(이)가 1408년 12월 12일 영국 노르섬프토실르 쿠르티홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edwin Sargent III(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Frances Morgan(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1432년 영국 노르섬프토실르에서 Eliza Braybrook(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1509년 5월 14일 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 쿠르티홀에서 100세의 나이로 사망했다.
